metadata_checks.comments: Title, DOI, year, paper type, and tax scope all match; the paper squarely studies carbon-tax and fossil-fuel-tax support.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Believes the policy would reduce GHG emissions
determinant_checks.comment: Direct predictor of support for the carbon-tax outcome; consistent with the paper’s main mechanism.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Believes the policy would reduce air pollution
determinant_checks.comment: Directly linked to acceptability of the carbon-tax outcome and reported as a major support predictor.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Believes the policy would have positive econ. effects
determinant_checks.comment: Included in the paper’s support regressions; a weaker but still tax-relevant predictor.
Core fairness channel; the paper states this lowers support for the tax.
Direct self-interest channel; clearly reduces support for the carbon-tax outcome.
Fairness is tightly linked to support; the paper reports a very strong raw correlation.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: cash transfers to the poorest households / cash transfers to constrained households
determinant_checks.comment: Direct carbon-tax revenue-recycling design; more progressive recycling increases support.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: fund environmental infrastructures, subsidize low-carbon technologies, or reduce income taxes
determinant_checks.comment: Directly tax-specific revenue-use design with substantially higher support than equal rebates.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: equal cash transfers to all households
determinant_checks.comment: Direct carbon-tax design variant; lower support than progressive recycling, so it belongs.
Direct tax-recycling variant; low support makes it relevant to tax acceptability.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Car-dependent respondents are less supportive of taxes on fossil fuels and carbon taxes with cash transfers
determinant_checks.comment: Direct fuel-tax burden correlate; clearly tax-specific and policy-relevant.
Policy-explanation video directly raises carbon-tax support and perceived fairness.
Combined video includes the tax-policy module and shows the strongest positive effect on carbon-tax support.
General climate-impacts information mostly shifts concern/private behavior; the paper says it barely moves public-policy support.
determinant_checks.suggested_correction: Remove from the strict tax-determinant table.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Believes the high-income earners will lose
determinant_checks.comment: Heterogeneous across countries; positive in several cases but not uniform.

missing_tax_determinants.outcome_variable: Support for taxes on fossil fuels and carbon taxes with cash transfers
missing_tax_determinants.evidence_quote: Car-dependent respondents are less supportive of taxes on fossil fuels and carbon taxes with cash transfers
missing_tax_determinants.page_or_section: Section III.C; Supplemental Appendix Figure A8
missing_tax_determinants.comment: Direct fuel-tax exposure proxy omitted from the extraction.

missing_tax_determinants.outcome_variable: Support for taxes on fossil fuels and carbon taxes with cash transfers
missing_tax_determinants.evidence_quote: Conditional on car usage, high gas expenses matter only marginally
missing_tax_determinants.comment: Weak but direct fuel-cost exposure variable; should be flagged if aiming for completeness.

final_recommendation_summary: Keep the paper in the strict tax corpus. Most extracted determinants are supported and tax-specific. Remove the climate-impacts treatment row as too broad for a tax-determinant table, and consider adding direct fuel-exposure correlates such as car dependence and high gas expenses.
